name: Castle of Segura de la Sierra from Orcera - N. P. of the Sierras of Cazorla, Segura and las Villas
name_original: Castillo de Segura de la Sierra desde Orcera - P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Hiking around Orcera offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Segura mountains, part of the vast Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. The region features a mountainous topography with peaks like El Picorzo and Peñalta, fertile valleys, and waterways such as the Orcera River and Amurjo River. Hikers can explore pine forests, extensive olive groves, and a variety of endemic plant species. The landscape provides varied elevation changes and panoramic views across the natural park. Best hiking trails around Orcera The most popular hiking route is Moralejos de Arriba from Seguro de la Sierra - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 47 minutes to complete. This circular route traverses the Natural Park, offering varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castle of Segura de la Sierra from Orcera - N. P. of the Sierras of Cazorla, Segura and las Villas, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.6 km) path. This trail leads to the historic Castle of Segura de la Sierra, offering cultural interest and panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Peñalta Summit Trail from Benatae, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through the Peñalta summit, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Orcera is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and olive groves, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Orcera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Orcera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Orcera?
A: Orcera offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 routes available for exploration. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Orcera?
A: The Sierra de Segura mountains, where Orcera is located, offer pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters can bring cooler temperatures to the mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the general terrain and difficulty level of hikes in Orcera?
A: The terrain around Orcera is mountainous and diverse, featuring towering peaks, fertile valleys, pine forests, and extensive olive groves. You'll encounter varied elevation changes. Most routes are classified as moderate, with 29 moderate trails, 5 easy trails, and 7 difficult trails available, ensuring options for all experience levels.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I discover on hikes in Orcera?
A: Hiking in Orcera offers stunning natural features and panoramic viewpoints. You can explore peaks like El Picorzo and Peñalta, which provide expansive vistas of the Sierra de Segura. Waterways such as the Orcera River, Amurjo River, and the Cascades of La Hueta are also notable. Strategic points like the Torres de Santa Catalina and the Zona de la Atalaya offer breathtaking views of the Orcera valley and surrounding mountains.

Q: What historical sites and attractions can I visit near Orcera's hiking trails?
A: Near Orcera's hiking trails, you can explore several historical and cultural attractions. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5239771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Segura de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a primary cultural highlight, offering spectacular views. The ancient Almohad watchtowers, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torres de Santa Catalina&lt;/a&gt;, also provide panoramic vistas. The village of Orcera itself retains traditional Andalusian charm with narrow streets and whitewashed houses.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Orcera?
A: The hiking routes in Orcera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Peñalta, and the opportunity to explore both natural landscapes and historical sites like the Castle of Segura de la Sierra.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Orcera?
A: Yes, Orcera offers several easy hiking trails suitable for families. These routes typically feature gentler inclines and shorter distances, allowing families to enjoy the natural beauty of the Sierra de Segura at a comfortable pace. While specific family-friendly routes are not listed here, the 5 easy trails available are a good starting point.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Orcera?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, which encompasses Orcera. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please check specific park regulations for any restricted areas.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Orcera?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, Orcera is a rural area, and parking is generally available near popular starting points for hikes. It is advisable to check local signage or maps for designated parking areas, especially when visiting during peak seasons.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Orcera?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Orcera. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984773935/moralejos-de-arriba-from-seguro-de-la-sierra-circular-through-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moralejos de Arriba from Seguro de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a well-regarded circular trail that traverses the Natural Park, offering varied landscapes without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Orcera?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Orcera offers 7 difficult trails. These routes typically involve significant elevation gains, longer distances, and more rugged terrain, providing a rewarding experience within the Sierra de Segura mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983401625/penalta-summit-trail-from-benatae" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalta Summit Trail from Benatae&lt;/a&gt;, while moderate, offers a good climb with rewarding views.

Q: Are there any spots for wild swimming or cooling off after a hike in Orcera?
A: Yes, the region around Orcera offers natural spots for cooling off. The Piscina de Amurjo, a natural swimming pool formed by the Orcera River, provides a tranquil place for relaxation and a refreshing dip after a hike.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Orcera?
A: Orcera and its surrounding villages offer various amenities for hikers. You'll find traditional Andalusian charm in the villages, with local establishments where you can enjoy regional cuisine. Accommodation options range from rural guesthouses to hotels, providing comfortable stays after a day on the trails.
tips:
This defense fortification was built on the remains of a previous fortress by the Order of Santiago, which placed the center of the Commandery of Castile there.
It has suffered throughout history various processes of abandonment and inconsideration. In the 1960s a restoration took place that, with greater or lesser success, has allowed us to fit out this historic building as a public space. At the end of the 20th century and the beginning of the 21st century, it underwent another adaptation process that, also, with greater or lesser success, has led us to reflect on museographic intervention.
